project.name: project-oracle-frontend
project.context: Indonesian stock risk console for Oracle screener, quant-confirmed signal review, and active tracking.

designPhilosophy: Author a defensive market-operations surface that makes selectivity visible before scanner volume.

distinctiveMoves: Use a risk-console composition with an instrument-grid background instead of a generic hero-plus-cards layout.
Surface defensive mode before KPI counts so weak win-rate and negative PnL become operating context.
Separate watchlist radar from executable signals through copy, framing, and semantic color shifts.
Use emerald as the protection rail, copper as caution, ember as loss/risk, and ice-blue as analysis metadata.

conceptualAnchor.sourceDomain: industrial risk instrumentation and trading-floor control systems
conceptualAnchor.rationale: The operator is losing money from low-selectivity signals, so the interface should behave like a protection console that filters noise before inviting action.
conceptualAnchor.derivedTokenLogic: Dark instrument neutrals create low-glare persistence; emerald marks protection and confirmed action; copper marks caution; ember marks loss; ice-blue marks analysis metadata.
conceptualAnchor.visualRiskBudget: Use squared panels, instrument-grid rhythm, and one full-width risk band; avoid decorative surfaces that make scanner volume feel celebratory.
conceptualAnchor.motionRiskBudget: Use short reveals and state emphasis only; avoid ambient motion while the system is in defensive selection mode.
cohesionChecks: Risk state appears before ticker counts.
Radar and execution surfaces use visibly different hierarchy.
Rejected quant setups cannot appear as actionable BUY signals.

tokenSystem.aliasingStrategy: Primitive values define color, spacing, radius, and motion. Semantic aliases define intent. Component tokens consume semantic aliases only.

tokenLayerRoles.primitive: Raw values for color, spacing, radius, typography, and motion.
tokenLayerRoles.semantic: Intent-bearing aliases such as background, panel, accent, positive, danger, muted, and focus.
tokenLayerRoles.component: Section, card, chip, button, and table tokens that consume semantic aliases.

colorTruth.intent: Keep the palette restrained and desk-like, with emerald protection rails and copper caution accents over stable market semantics.

mutationRules.mobile: Put risk mode, pending actions, and active positions first, then disclose radar and history lower in the stack.
mutationRules.tablet: Retain two-column grouping for radar and signals while keeping the risk console full-width above them.
mutationRules.desktop: Expose the full risk-console grid with radar, controls, signal review, portfolio, and history visible without tab switching.

motionSystem.purpose: Use motion for continuity, urgency, and authored polish without compromising performance.
motionSystem.choreography: Fast instrument reveals and border emphasis on actionable cards; avoid ambient motion that rewards over-scanning.

viewportBehavior.mobile: Signal cards become stacked narratives with chart, duration, price plan, reasoning, and actions in order of urgency.
viewportBehavior.tablet: Tables selectively remain tabular, while signal and radar surfaces stay card-based.
viewportBehavior.desktop: Cards and tables coexist inside a wide command grid with persistent section identity.

surfacePlan: Header risk band with actions and operating context
Risk console strip that names defensive or normal mode
Radar panel for screener anomalies and manual watchlist
componentGraph: DashboardPage -> HeaderBand, OverviewStrip, RadarPanel, SignalBoard, PortfolioPanel, HistoryPanel
designExecutionHandoff.signatureMoveRationale: The risk-console framing visually distinguishes scanner noise from executable signals and turns poor recent performance into a visible selectivity constraint.

validBoldSignals: clear semantic separation of radar versus execution
authored typography and protection-first accent budget
reviewRubric.reportingRule: Every genericity finding must name the exact drift signal.

contextHygiene.continuityRule: Continuity applies to Oracle product behavior and data surfaces, not to the previous monolithic UI styling.

experiencePrinciples: Product copy must stay fully Indonesian on user-facing surfaces.
Daily screener radar must never imply one-day profit certainty.
Signal execution surfaces should feel strict and operational without becoming noisy.
Weak win-rate or negative average PnL should activate defensive language instead of encouraging more trades.
Every current data surface must survive the redesign.
Responsive behavior must reorganize content, not merely shrink it.

uiSignalReasons: dashboard currently renders anomalies, watchlist, signals, portfolio, history, and stats
current UI is monolithic and mixed-language

driftSignals: mixed Indonesian and English user copy
